title: U.S. auto sales rose just over 1% for the first half of the year - JD Power
content:
[GM Canada Technical Centre campus in Markham, Ontario, Canada.]
JHVEPhoto

New vehicle sales in the U.S. are expected to rise to 3.6% year over year in June to 1,363,800 units on an adjusted basis, according to a joint forecast from JD Power and GlobalData. The seasonally adjusted annualized rate for total new-vehicle sales is expected to be 16.5M units, up 0.8M units from June 2025. The comparison to a year ago was impacted by the pulling forward of demand last year due to tariffs. "Last year, consumers were reacting to the perceived risk of higher prices from vehicle tariffs, and the resulting volatility makes simple year-over-year comparisons murky," highlighted JD Power's Thomas King.

Total new-vehicle sales in Q2 2026 are projected to reach 4,226,600 units, a year-over-year increase of 0.7% from Q2 2025. Total new-vehicle sales for the first half of 2026 are projected to reach 8,245,700 units, an increase of 1.2%.

The average transaction price of a new vehicle has increased to $46,387, an increase of 0.8% from a year ago, while average monthly finance payments have climbed 3.4% to $813, the highest ever for the month of June. It was noted that the cost of financing a new vehicle continues to ease, though not by enough to neutralize the structural affordability pressures weighing on car buyers. Auto manufacturers are also leaning harder on discounts to keep buyers in the market. Notably, average incentive spending per vehicle was up almost 13% from a year ago to $3,217.

title: Honda CEO Survives Loss Backlash
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Honda Motor (NYSE:HMC) CEO Toshihiro Mibe won shareholder backing for reappointment to the board after apologizing for the automaker's first annual loss since going public about 70 years ago.

At Honda's annual meeting Friday, Mibe apologized to shareholders for the significant concern and inconvenience caused by the loss. The company was hit by more than $9 billion in restructuring costs tied to its electric vehicle business.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 5 Warning Signs with HMC. Is HMC fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Mibe said Honda took the EV-related writedown because of weaker-than-expected U.S. demand for battery-powered vehicles and the rollback of EV subsidies. He said moving ahead with some planned EVs could have left the auto business in the red for at least 5 years, and possibly as long as 7.

Shareholders still approved Mibe's reappointment, along with 10 other board nominees. Proxy advisers Glass Lewis and ISS had recommended backing all directors.

Honda Motor (HMC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HMC]) shareholders voted in favor of reappointing CEO Toshihiro Mibe to the board after he apologized for the automaker's weak results at its annual meeting ​on Friday.

The company last month posted its first annual loss since it went public about 70 years ago, hit by over $9B in restructuring costs for its electric vehicle business.

"I would like to ​express my deepest apologies to our shareholders for the significant concern and inconvenience caused ​by the net loss recorded in the previous fiscal year's financial results," Mibe ⁠told shareholders at the meeting, as reported by _Reuters_.

The CEO said Honda (HMC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HMC]) decided on its EV-related writedown amid the rollback of EV subsidies and lower-than-expected market share of battery-powered cars in the U.S.

If the company decided to go ahead with selling its planned EVs, "it would mean the automotive ‌business ⁠itself staying in the red for at least five years, possibly as long as seven," Mibe said.

title: Ford wins J.D. Power's top spot, CEO calls it a 'breathtaking achievement'
content:
J.D. Power named Ford (F) the top mainstream brand in its 2026 US Initial Quality Study, the first time that the American automaker reached number one in 16 years.

Ford CEO Jim Farley sits down with Yahoo Finance Senior Autos Reporter Pras Subramanian to explain why this accomplishment was four years in the making for the car manufacturer.

Video Transcript

00:00 Speaker A

This is a big day for Ford. It's, you know, we've worked really hard for four years to be an overnight success story. I mean, it it's been an incredible journey over the last several years. Uh we have completely transformed all of our plants and our quality operating system. and and people need, you know, I I'm sure they understand this, but I like to be specific. We make 85% of all vehicles in the US. So to beat Toyota

00:36 Speaker A

and and all the others that import most a lot of their vehicles from Asia, even our domestic competitors that that they import over a million vehicles a year with American workers, you know, an American company to do that is a breathtaking achievement. First time in 16 years. I'm I'm so proud of the team and this is an American success story.

01:08 Speaker B

You know, you mentioned, you know, besting brands like Toyota and Honda known for that quality level. What do you think flipped the switch there exactly? What that that sort of made this sort of happen with the with the initial quality survey here?

01:21 Speaker A

Well, we were in crisis and and you know, but we worked hard for the last four years. This is not a overnight success story for us. It may be new news to everyone else, but what really made the difference is a culture change at the company. We decided to put quality first. Now that seems really simple, but you can only imagine what that means in all of our plants. I we we manage quality as the highest priority in the company.

02:04 Speaker A

Um, you go into any of our plants in the US, you will see our workers completely obsessed with all the data. Every torque wrench is measured. We look at every defect and understand why it happened. We go all the way back to the suppliers and our engineers. We have armies of our engineers in our plants every day working with our factory workers to make the quality of our designs better. All of our supply chain team are in our suppliers looking at their quality. We really this is a story of paying attention to details,

02:44 Speaker A

knowing exactly what world class looks like. We knew exactly what it takes to beat Toyota and every part of the vehicle, you know, designing it, the parts that go into it and the assembly of the vehicle, and including all the software testing, you know, that is an obsession with quality is is really what happened here at Ford.

title: Yen Slide Could Hand Japan Automakers $5.8 Billion Boost
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Japan's weakening yen could hand the country's automakers a major earnings boost, even as policymakers try to slow the currency's slide near a 40-year low. Bloomberg estimates that Toyota Motor (NYSE:TM), Honda (NYSE:HMC), Nissan, Subaru, and Mazda could see a combined profit upside of roughly 934 billion, or $5.8 billion, if the yen stays near current levels. Toyota's May forecast assumed 150 per dollar, while the yen has been trading around 161, and the company estimates that every 1 decline in the currency adds 50 billion to operating profit. That currency gap could become a meaningful tailwind for Toyota, while Honda, Nissan, Subaru, and Mazda also used more conservative exchange-rate assumptions in their forecasts.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 4 Warning Sign with TM. Is TM fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The setup could become even stronger as energy and raw material costs ease following the U.S.Iran peace agreement. Expectations that the Strait of Hormuz could reopen have pushed oil prices sharply lower, with crude down more than 30% in yen terms from its late-April peak. Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Tatsuo Yoshida said the shift could become a major positive factor for companies such as Toyota and Honda, especially since they had already factored Middle East tensions into full-year forecasts. Toyota had previously warned that higher material costs and production cuts tied to Middle East instability could reduce earnings by 670 billion, but its 3 trillion operating profit forecast could possibly be revised higher, with Bloomberg-compiled analyst estimates averaging 4 trillion.

The impact outside automakers looks more mixed. Airlines such as ANA Holdings and Japan Airlines could benefit from jet fuel prices falling to less than half of their March peak, but the weaker yen still creates pressure on dollar-linked costs. ANA had estimated that Middle East tensions and higher fuel costs would cut operating profit by about 60 billion this fiscal year, while import-heavy businesses such as Nitori Holdings remain exposed to yen weakness. A Tokyo Shoko Research survey showed 40.7% of companies said the late-May exchange rate near 159 per dollar had a negative impact, suggesting investors may start separating companies that can pass on higher costs from those that cannot.

title: Ford CEO on JD Power win: This 'overnight success' was actually 4 years in the making
content:
Ford (F) was named the top mainstream brand in JD Power's 2026 US Initial Quality Study — the industry standard for vehicle quality rankings — for the first time in 16 years.

The result, released Thursday, pushes Ford past stalwarts like Toyota (TM) and Honda (HMC) that have dominated in quality and reliability. Among mass market brands this year, JD Power said Ford topped Nissan (7201.T) and General Motors' Buick in terms of fewest problems in the first 90 days of ownership.

Only premium brands like Porsche (DRPRF) and Hyundai's Genesis finished higher across the entire industry, leaving Ford third overall.Ford only trails Porsche and Genesis in initial quality, making it the top mainstream brand in JD Power's survey.·JD Power

"Oh boy, this is a big day for Ford," CEO Jim Farley said in an interview with Yahoo Finance. "We've worked really hard for four years to be an overnight success story. It's been an incredible journey over the last several years. We have completely transformed all of our plants and our quality operating system."

The turnaround is quite a surprise given how far back Ford had fallen. Ford ranked No. 15 among mainstream brands as recently as 2023. Then this year Ford posted the largest year-over-year improvement of any mainstream brand, with seven of its 10 models tested ranked in the top three of their segments — the highest share of any automaker.

The F-150, Mustang and F-Series Super Duty each won their segments for a second straight year.

Farley also said this was a win for US manufacturing, Ford making the most cars in the US of any domestic manufacturer. "We make 85% of all vehicles in the US," he said. "To beat Toyota and all the others that import most of a lot of their vehicles from Asia — even our domestic competitors that import over a million vehicles a year — with American workers, an American company, to do that is a breathtaking achievement."

F

Go deeper with AlphaSpace

14.34 +0.49 (+3.58%)

As of 12:12:58 PM EDT. Market Open.

Typically, American brands have suffered in quality versus brands from Japan and Germany. Farley says Ford needed a culture shift to get on par with those rivals. He attributed the climb to culture rather than any single fix. Critics argue Ford took too long to fix its quality woes, but Farley said changing its culture required time to fix, as well as making attention to detail a top issue.

"You go into any of our plants in the US, you'll see our workers completely obsessed with all the data. Every torque wrench is measured. We look at every defect and understand why it happened," he said.

For investors, this not only provided a benefit on the sales floor, but also with Ford's bottom line. Ford recorded lower warranty costs in 2025 and expects the trend to continue this year, a meaningful signal for a company that has been hammered by recall and warranty expenses. "Our warranty costs are going down, our recall costs are going down," Farley said. "So this will be a big financial story for the company too."

Story Continues

Detroit, Michigan USA - 13 January 2026 - Ford CEO Jim Farley spoke as his company showed its off-road vehicles at the Detroit Auto Show. (Photo by: Jim West/UCG/Universal Images Group via Getty Images)·UCG via Getty Images

Another big financial story is US consumers and affordability issues, and for automakers, President Trump's tariffs introduced last year continue to ripple through the industry as Americans strain to make car payments.

Despite its best efforts, Farley argues Ford has been penalized despite its domestic footprint: "Even though we make almost all of our vehicles in the US, we make more vehicles than anyone else, we export more than anyone else, we have the most factory workers in the US — so we're obviously the most American company. And to have a big tariff bill didn't make sense to us."

This is because it has parts sourcing and finished components coming across the border from Canada and Mexico, and vice versa.

It's why Ford views the coming renegotiation of the US-Mexico-Canada trade pact (USMCA), due this summer and fall, as "hugely important." Ford wants stronger local-content requirements and reasonable tariffs on inputs like aluminum. "We want more companies to look like Ford," he said.A 2025 Ford Maverick Lobo (4x4) is displayed during media day of the Detroit Auto Show in Detroit, Michigan, U.S. January 14, 2026. REUTERS/Rebecca Cook·REUTERS / REUTERS

Aside from tariff reform, Farley pointed to a wave of cheaper models to address affordability issues. "We're going to be launching five or six new affordable vehicles," he said, "and the first one is transformational. It'll be our less-than-$30,000 new electric truck coming out next year." He likened the opening to the runaway success of the Maverick, sold out since its launch four years ago.

It's also how Ford will counter Chinese rivals. Farley noted Ford's "skunkworks" EV team in California, and its battery-cost breakthrough with efficiency.

"BYD vertically integrates — they make their own batteries," he said, estimating that gave the Chinese giant a 20% to 30% cost edge.

"We were able to get 30% efficiency compared to them in our hardware, and that allowed our battery to be 30% smaller. That means we are fully competitive with BYD on our battery cost. We did it through innovation — not through support from the Chinese government."

Pras Subramanian is Lead Auto Reporter for Yahoo Finance. You can follow him on X and on Instagram.

title: Tesla leads Cars.com's American-Made Index again, even as EV sales slump
content:
For a second time, Tesla (TSLA) heads Cars.com's list of the most American-made vehicles, a paradox that comes as the US EV market it helped build shrinks in a post-tax-credit world.

Cars.com said on Tuesday that for the sixth straight year, the Tesla Model 3 claimed the No. 1 spot on its American-Made Index (AMI). The Model Y followed at No. 2 for a second consecutive year, giving Tesla the top two spots of the index.

Stellantis's (STLA) Jeep took the next two spots with the Gladiator and Grand Cherokee, while Honda (HMC) placed five vehicles in the top 10 — the Ridgeline, Odyssey, Accord, Passport, and Acura MDX.

The 2026 list named 86 vehicles, down from 99 a year earlier. Cars.com attributed the drop to automakers trimming their lineups as President Trump's tariffs introduced last year worked their way through the industry.

"This year's list represents the ongoing evolution and diversification of American-made vehicles," Cars.com AMI lead researcher Patrick Masterson said in the report. "Notably, nearly two-thirds of this year's list was produced by foreign automakers, about the same as in 2025, while the Big Three still hold approximately one-third of the vehicles."The list of vehicles on Cars.com's 2026 American-Made Index.·Cars.com

Cars.com weighed each eligible vehicle on a 100-point scale across five factors: location of final assembly, the percentage of US and Canadian parts as reported under the American Automobile Labeling Act, US manufacturing workforce relative to output, and the countries of origin for available powertrains.

The final assembly location is a major factor. Cars.com said a vehicle has to roll off one of 48 US plants run by 13 automaker groups to qualify at all, which is why imports never appear, and why even a US-built model loses points for imported volume.

Tesla's dominance of the index comes at a challenging time for it and other EV makers. New EV sales fell 27% year over year in the first quarter of 2026 to around 216.4K units, according to Cox Automotive's Kelley Blue Book. EVs made up just 5.8% of all new-vehicle sales — flat from the prior quarter, but a steep dip from the 10.6% market share hit in the third quarter of 2025.

Expiration of the $7,500 federal EV tax credit at the end of Q3 was the main culprit, and demand has sagged ever since. Many automakers have throttled back production, with some major brands posting Q1 EV sales declines of 60% to 70% or more. In fact, the AMI share of electrified vehicles slipped from 30% to 24%, and the number of pure EVs on the list nearly halved, falling from 11 to five.

Story Continues

Tesla's US sales dropped more than 8% in the quarter, by Cox's estimate. Yet the company actually gained ground as rivals fell faster, with the Model Y and Model 3 accounting for 51% of all US EV sales.

That concentration helps explain Tesla's staying power on the index. The automaker now relies almost entirely on two high-volume vehicles, both built domestically and both heavy on US-sourced content. Cars.com noted that this year's top 10 carried the highest average domestic parts content since it overhauled its methodology in 2020.

Foreign automakers built nearly two-thirds of this year's list, with Toyota (TM) contributing 14 vehicles and Honda 13 — more than any Detroit automaker.A Cars.com consumer survey conducted in May 2026 finds tariffs are still impacting the car-buying process in a profound way.·Cars.com AMI Survey, May 2026

And tariffs are reshaping how shoppers think, and act, too. In a Cars.com consumer survey conducted in early May, almost half of respondents said tariffs are a concern while shopping for a new car, and 42% said tariffs have made them more likely to buy an American-made vehicle.

Two-thirds said they would consider an American car if tariffs lowered its cost. The pressure is hitting brand loyalty too, with the share of shoppers who claim to stick to one brand falling more than nine percentage points from last year.

Meanwhile, 57% said they would pay more for a vehicle that supports US jobs, and 45% ranked price as the single most important factor in a purchase.

Pras Subramanian is Lead Auto Reporter for Yahoo Finance. You can follow him on X and on Instagram.

title: Nissan Director Voted Out in Rare Public Standoff With Renault
content:
(Bloomberg) -- Nissan Motor Co. shareholders rejected the reappointment of Motoo Nagai to the board, ending the tenure of an influential outside director after he lost the support of key stakeholder Renault SA, following a rare public standoff at Japan's typically staid annual meetings.

Most Read from Bloomberg

SpaceX Falls for Third Day, Erases $600 Billion in Market Value Oracle Cut 21,000 Jobs in 12 Months, Says AI Replaced Some Roles Stocks Fall as SpaceX's Rout Offsets US-Iran Hopes: Markets Wrap Meta Invests $900 Million in Cred, Taps Founder to Head WhatsApp Iran Says Hormuz Closed Again as Talks With US Set to Open

Chief Executive Officer Ivan Espinosa announced the results at Nissan's annual meeting on Tuesday, and confirmed that 11 other directors had reached the majority needed to be appointed, or reappointed.

Renault, which holds 15% of voting rights, had planned to abstain from voting for Nagai, people with knowledge of its plans had said. Nagai, a former banker, played a pivotal role during the 2018 ouster of former Chairman Carlos Ghosn and the subsequent appointment of executives.

Nagai, 72, supported the ultimately unsuccessful merger talks between Nissan and Honda Motor Co. in early 2025, and spent his career at Mizuho Financial Group Inc., Nissan's top creditor. He's the only director serving on the nomination, compensation and audit committees, giving him sway over the appointment of executives at Nissan. The ex-banker's ouster represents Renault's biggest power move at Nissan since ceding much of its influence over the Japanese carmaker in 2023.

Renault's concerns over Nagai also focused on whether he could be considered an independent director, given his long association with Nissan, given that he joined as statutory auditor in 2014 and became a board member in 2019.

New nominee Junichi Shinbo, also a former Mizuho banker, was voted in by shareholders at the meeting. Renault had also planned to abstain voting for him, according to the people, who asked not to be identified discussing internal matters. Their links to the bank, as well as Nagai's long tenure at Nissan, brought their independence into question, they added.

Renault owns roughly 36% of Nissan's stock but exercises fewer voting rights following a renegotiated alliance agreement between the companies in 2023. Renault became a major stakeholder when it saved the carmaker from bankruptcy more than a quarter century ago, sending in Ghosn who eventually became CEO of both companies.

Several disgruntled shareholders voiced their disapproval at this year's meeting, taking jabs at Nissan's executives, pointing to the dismal performance of its share price and a lack of compelling models. Nissan's stock is down 44% since the end of 2023.

Story Continues

One investor openly questioned the independence of Nagai and Shinbo, and opposed the reappointment of Espinosa in a motion that was ultimately denied on legal grounds. "The biggest issue for shareholders attending this meeting is the stock price," the shareholder said. "Most shareholders here are extremely unhappy."

Nissan has been struggling to regain its footing, having posted net losses for the past two fiscal years, although it is forecasting a return to profit for the current period through March 2027. Sales declined 4.9% to ¥12 trillion ($74.3 billion) in the latest year as the carmaker seeks to refresh its aging lineup. The carmaker has ¥4.4 trillion in debt and rating agencies have cut its creditworthiness status to junk.

BYD Co. and other Chinese carmakers have erased Nissan's early lead in affordable electric vehicles, while Toyota Motor Corp.'s hybrid strategy is leaving Nissan and its rival Honda even further behind. The two smaller car companies almost merged last year, but abandoned talks after failing to agree on a structure.

A total of 12 board members, including Espinosa, were up for a vote at the annual meeting, including three new directors. Two long-serving members stepped down.

In a letter to shareholders, Andrew House, the Nissan director serving as chair of the nomination committee, said the committee "concluded that Mr. Nagai's independence is not compromised and that his reelection is in the best interests of the company and its shareholders."

Proxy advisory firms Institutional Shareholder Services Inc. and Glass Lewis & Co., which advise institutional investors on proposals ahead of annual meetings, have recommended that shareholders vote against Nagai's reappointment. "Nominee Motoo Nagai is not independent," Glass Lewis wrote in its report, published June 3.

When Nissan, and then eventually Japanese prosecutors, began investigating Ghosn's compensation almost a decade ago, Nagai was closely involved from the beginning.

After the Ghosn saga, which mostly ended when he fled Japan in late 2019 and escaped trial, Renault and Nissan were mired in a power struggle. In 2023, they agreed to reshape the alliance, with the French carmaker committing to reducing its holding in Nissan to 15% from 43% to rebalance their lopsided capital ties. After the partial sale of its shares, Renault still controls about a third of Nissan's stock.

As management turmoil engulfed Nissan in the years following Ghosn's ouster, Nagai emerged as a powerful broker of executive appointments. He was a key player in the departure of former COO Ashwani Gupta, and was closely involved in the selection of Espinosa, people familiar with the matter have said.

"Even in a challenging environment of intensified competition, our efforts to improve the business have borne fruit," Espinosa said, repeating a previous pledge to regain profitability during the fiscal year ending March 2027.

title: Chinese carmakers exploring Canada’s EV import quota, Joly says
content:
Investing.com -- Four Chinese automakers are examining ways to use Canada's low-tariff import quota for electric vehicles and are considering domestic manufacturing partnerships, Industry Minister Melanie Joly said Monday.

Joly traveled to China last week and met with officials from BYD Co., Chery Automobile Co., Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., and Shanghai Launch Automotive Technical Co.

Her trip followed Prime Minister Mark Carney's January visit to Beijing, where he announced a trade agreement that permits up to 49,000 Chinese-made EVs to enter Canada under a tariff of approximately 6%. The deal eliminated the additional 100% tariff imposed on the vehicles in 2024.

Under the agreement, Carney's government is seeking to attract Chinese investment into the Canadian auto sector.

Joly told reporters that all the companies she met with are "willing to explore creating joint ventures in Canada in order to produce in Canada."

She stated that such arrangements would need to meet specific conditions, including Canadian control of the joint venture and a localized supply chain.

"We need to make sure that we are able to do this right," she said, referencing her ministerial oversight of the Investment Canada Act, which permits her to set conditions on sensitive foreign investments in Canada.

Carney's government has held discussions with Stellantis NV about building Chinese EVs in an idled plant in Brampton, Ontario. The vehicles would potentially be produced through Stellantis' joint venture with Chinese firm Zhejiang Leapmotor Technology Co.

After news of the talks emerged, Joly said she would reject "cars in a kit," where the parts are built overseas and shipped to Canada for final assembly.

Chinese-made EVs began entering Canada under the new low-tariff quota in May. The majority are Tesla Inc. cars made at the company's Shanghai factory.

Last month, Honda Motor Co. indefinitely suspended plans to build a C$15 billion battery supply chain in Alliston, Ontario.

"I'm confident good news is coming," Joly said. "I know that there's a lot of anxiety in the auto sector, but to those working in Alliston, we have your back and we're working with the company."

title: Driving an EV in These US States Saves You the Most Money
content:
(Bloomberg) -- Nearly $1,500 a year. That's roughly the fuel savings at the moment between a current gas car and the typical electric vehicle charged at a US home.

Most Read from Bloomberg

Read the 14-Point Draft Memorandum Between the US and Iran US-Iran Nuclear Talks Stall Over Lebanon Before They Begin Iran Says Hormuz Is Closed Again as Talks With US Set to Proceed DOJ Rejects Judge Request to Certify $1.8 Billion Fund Nixed Iran Floats 'Insurance Fees' and Asserts Control Over Hormuz

Across the country, that number is fully tilted in favor of driving electric, but it varies by state. In some places, both gas and electricity are pricey (see: California). But in a huge swath of the American West – nine US states, including Arizona, Utah and Washington – consumers pay more than average for gas and less than average for electricity, according to a Bloomberg analysis of fuel prices and household power rates.

These are the sweet spots for EV adoption – only made sweeter since the Iran War spiked prices at the pump. Though they have come down somewhat in recent weeks, they remain well above pre-war levels.

"Given how high gas prices are, you're doing a lot of savings everywhere, but these places are pretty good," said Corey Cantor, research director at the Zero Emission Transportation Association, a nonprofit advocacy group. "It's definitely a huge selling point."

Consider Washington, which has the third-highest gas prices in the country and some of the cheapest electricity, thanks largely to hydroelectric power and public utilities. Assuming a typical American driving distance, swapping a gas car for an EV, in this case, would save $2,346 a year. Oregon isn't far behind at $2,057.

To be sure, fuel is only one of the costs to consider in a vehicle. EVs, on average, still cost more than gas cars – though that spread has been shrinking steadily – and they depreciate more quickly. On the flip side, they require far less in the way of maintenance. Fuel, however, is the most persistent cost and when buying a car, shoppers often underestimate it, focusing overly much on the sticker price.

"You see gas signs everywhere, but you don't necessarily see dollars per kilowatt signs, unless you're using an app," Cantor explained. "You kind of have to crunch the numbers yourself."

That said, many US consumers appear to be doing the math just fine on their own. EV market share is highest in places where gas is dear and power is cheap. California tops the list with 16% of new cars purchased this year through April powered entirely by batteries. But fuel savings is accelerating EVs in places that don't have incentives or deep-blue voting patterns. Nevada, is third on the list with 11% EV share and Arizona is eighth when it comes to EV interest, in part because its power prices are far lower than those of the average American household.

Story Continues

"Those are the types of places that give you optimism for long-term EV transition," Cantor said, "because it's really just market forces at work."

Corey Crawford, a sound engineer in Portland, swapped his old Honda CR-V for a used Nissan Ariya, a fully electric model, in the fall. His wife wanted to trade her sporty BMW coupe for another gas car, but when gas prices spiked in April, she settled on an EV as well, the new Toyota C-HR.

With a home charger, the couple's monthly fuel bill has gone from about $200 to $25.

"It's been great," Crawford said. "You drive to Costco and you see people lined up at the fuel pumps and it's just like: 'I'm so glad I never have to do that again.'"

EV sales in the US are expected to decline 19% this year, according to a new report from BloombergNEF. Still, global sales are predicted to surge by 11% and BNEF said the recent turmoil in oil markets has boosted consumer interest in kicking the gas habit, particularly in the market for used vehicles.

Nearly one-third of drivers considering an EV cited fuel savings as their rationale, according to a May survey from JD Power. The same survey found EV interest surged in May, with 26% of car shoppers reporting they were "very likely" to go electric, an increase of 3 percentage points from the prior year.

To be sure, electricity prices have risen as well recently, as AI data centers hoover up an unprecedented amount of power. But the increase – an 8.6% hike in the average retail rate in the past 12 months – pales in comparison to the spike in gasoline prices, primarily because fossil fuels are a small and declining share of America's power generation. Most of a US power bill goes to transmission and customer service; only about one quarter to one-third is tied to fuel costs. And as renewable energy gushed in recent years, oil and natural gas now comprise less than half of that fuel. The wave of green energy is particularly strong in Western states inundated with sun and wind.

At the other end of the EV equation, there are four states where drivers pay less than average for gas and more than average for electricity: Maryland, Ohio, Rhode Island and Wisconsin. However, on average, an EV in those places still saves $1,217 a year in fuel costs.

The EV advantage tends to be slightest in New England where power prices run hot and gas prices are still cool, at least relative to much of the country. An EV in Rhode Island, for example, saves less than $1,000 in fuel at the moment.

title: QuantumScape (QS) And Honda Enter Multi Year Solid State Battery Research Deal
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

QuantumScape and Honda have entered a multi year joint research agreement focused on solid state battery technologies. The agreement follows an extended period of validation work by Honda on QuantumScape's technology. The partnership targets potential use across Honda's electric vehicles, motorcycles, and power equipment platforms.

QuantumScape, traded on NasdaqGS:QS, is moving into a new phase with this Honda research agreement, shifting from being evaluated to actively co developing solid state battery technology with a global automaker. The stock last closed at $8.04, with a return of 13.4% over the past week and 87.4% over the past year. Over a longer horizon, QuantumScape's share price is up 17.5% over three years and down 72.2% over five years, which highlights the volatility that early investors have experienced.

For investors, this new collaboration reflects external validation of QuantumScape's technology and its potential relevance to multiple end markets. The company's shares are currently down 27.3% year to date and up 4.8% over the past month, so expectations around execution and timing remain an important consideration as this joint research progresses.

📰 Beyond the headline: 3 risks and 2 things going right for QuantumScape that every investor should see.

This Honda agreement pushes QuantumScape further into the role of a technology licensor partnering directly with large original equipment manufacturers. For a company still pre revenue and focused on solid state lithium metal batteries, having a second global auto group move from testing into a multi year joint program adds industrial context around its QSE 5 platform and manufacturing know how. The focus on co developing production processes, rather than only cell performance, lines up with QuantumScape's aim to supply designs and process blueprints rather than build its own gigafactories.

How This Fits Into The QuantumScape Narrative

The news supports the narrative that QuantumScape's capital light licensing model can scale through deep partnerships, adding Honda alongside existing collaborators on pilot lines and process blueprints. Relying on multi year research programs with large automakers can also stretch timelines, which could challenge expectations around when licensing and royalty revenues start to offset current losses. The potential use of QuantumScape technology in motorcycles, power equipment and energy storage is only partially reflected in the existing focus on auto and broader mobility, so some non automotive optionality may not be fully captured.

Story Continues

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story.Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for QuantumScape to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

⚠️ QuantumScape is still loss making, with sizeable adjusted EBITDA losses and ongoing R&D and capital expenditure, so delays in turning partnerships like Honda into licensing and royalty streams could extend heavy cash use. ⚠️ The business depends on a small group of large partners in a competitive battery space that includes players like CATL, Panasonic and Solid Power, so changes in partner priorities or competing technologies remain important risks. 🎁 A second major automaker moving beyond testing into joint research, after extensive benchmarking, supports the view that QuantumScape's solid state platform has technical advantages that are relevant to real world vehicle programs. 🎁 The collaboration fits QuantumScape's licensing model, with scope for applications across electric vehicles, motorcycles and power equipment, which could broaden the company's potential royalty base if programs progress.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, watch how QuantumScape and Honda define concrete technical milestones, such as cell performance targets, durability metrics and manufacturing yields, and whether those are met on schedule. Any disclosure on paid development work, licensing terms or expansion of the relationship into commercial supply discussions will matter for the revenue story. Investors should also track progress with existing partners and competitors' solid state efforts to see whether QuantumScape's technology remains differentiated in a field that includes established battery suppliers and other solid state developers.

title: QS Stock Alert: QuantumScape Strikes a Solid-State Battery Deal With Honda
content:
A sign and office building for QuantumScape by JHVEPhoto via Shutterstock

QuantumScape (QS) has reportedly secured a partnership with Honda Motor Company (HMC) to supply or co-develop solid-state battery technology for its future electric vehicles (EVs).

This agreement represents a significant commercial validation for QS, which has long been viewed as a high-potential but pre-revenue tech company attempting to commercialize lithium-metal solid-state cells.

More News from Barchart

Dear Nebius Stock Fans, Mark Your Calendars for June 22 ASTS Stock Pops as AST SpaceMobile Launches Bluebird Satellites Dear CoreWeave Stock Fans, Mark Your Calendars for June 22 Tired of missing midday reversals? The FREE Barchart Brief newsletter keeps you in the know. Sign up now!

Despite today's rally, QuantumScape stock is down more than 30% versus its year-to-date high. www.barchart.com

Why QuantumScape Stock Popped on Honda Deal

Teaming up with Honda positions QS alongside other major automakers pursuing next-generation battery chemistry as the EV industry seeks breakthroughs in energy density, charging speed, and safety. The timing of this partnership is notable given the broader EV landscape.

U.S. electric vehicle sales have recently rebounded to their strongest levels since the elimination of the $7,500 federal tax credit, with more than 85,000 units sold in May and average transaction prices declining for 11 consecutive months.

This recovery in demand, coupled with automakers' continued investment in battery technology advancement, creates a favorable backdrop for QuantumScape's commercialization efforts.

The Honda announcement is bullish for QS shares as it suggests that at least one major OEM believes solid-state tech is now approaching production readiness.

Why Solid-State Battery Technology Is Attractive

From a competitive standpoint, solid-state batteries promise roughly double the energy density of conventional lithium-ion cells while minimizing flammability risks tied to liquid electrolytes.

Honda has been actively pursuing solid-state battery development through its internal programs, making a partnership with QuantumScape a strategic complement rather than a full outsourcing of the technology.

This dual approach mirrors what other automakers have done, hedging internal R&D with external technology licensing arrangements.

Still, for those invested in QuantumScape shares, the Honda agreement addresses one of the most persistent concerns: whether the company can transition from lab achievements to commercial sales streams.

QS has historically struggled with skepticism about its timeline and manufacturing scalability. A binding agreement with a Tier 1 automaker like Honda Motor provides tangible evidence of commercial traction and could help de-risk the firm's path toward revenue generation.

Story Continues

Wall Street Remains Bearish on QS Shares for 2026

However, investors should remain cautious about the financial implications.

QuantumScape continues to operate at significant losses as it invests heavily in scaling its manufacturing processes, and the specific financial terms of the Honda agreement will determine how material this deal is to the company's near-term cash flow and long-term profitability.

Plus, the EV battery space remains intensely competitive, with established players like CATL and Samsung SDI also pursuing solid-state solutions, and the broader auto industry still grappling with elevated incentive spending of about $7,600 per EV sold in the U.S. market.

This is partly why Wall Street analysts continue to rate QS stock at "Moderate Sell," with a mean price target of $7.14 indicating potential downside of more than 10% from here. www.barchart.com

title: QuantumScape inks solid-state battery research deal with Honda, sending shares higher
content:
QuantumScape inks solid-state battery research deal with Honda, sending shares higher Proactive uses images sourced from Shutterstock

Quantumscape Corp (NYSE:QS) shares surged about 15% on Thursday after the solid-state battery developer announced a joint research agreement with Honda Motor (NYSE:HMC) subsidiary Honda R&D Co Ltd to advance its battery technology platform.

The multi-year collaboration will focus on solid-state battery development and related manufacturing processes, combining the expertise of both companies.

The agreement follows Honda's completion of a technology evaluation program with QuantumScape that included an in-depth assessment of the company's solid-state battery platform and benchmarking against competing technologies through a series of standard technical tests.

"QS technology demonstrated compelling and unique advantages during our evaluation," Atsushi Ogawa, chief operating officer of Honda R&D's Research Center of Excellence, said in a statement.

He added that the company sees potential for the technology across multiple applications, including automotive uses.

QuantumScape CEO Siva Sivaram said Honda's assessment represented "one of the most rigorous evaluations of our technology to date."

"This agreement reflects the growing confidence in QS solid-state lithium-metal batteries to enable safer, higher-density energy storage," Sivaram said.

The company develops solid-state lithium-metal batteries, which are viewed as a potential alternative to conventional lithium-ion batteries due to their potential for higher energy density and improved safety characteristics.

title: QuantumScape Announces Agreement with Honda on Solid-State Battery Technology
content:
Quantumscape Corporation

SAN JOSE, Calif., June 18, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- QuantumScape Corporation (NASDAQ: QS), a global leader in next-generation solid-state lithium-metal battery technology, today announced a joint research agreement with Honda R&D Co., Ltd., a subsidiary of Honda Motor Co., Ltd., one of the world's leading manufacturers of automobiles, motorcycles and power equipment, aimed at advancing the QS battery platform through the combined contributions and expertise of both parties. The joint program includes a multi-year plan focused on solid-state battery development and associated manufacturing processes.

The agreement follows Honda's successful completion of a technology evaluation agreement with QS, which included an in-depth, hands-on technical study of QS's solid-state technology platform as well as competitive benchmarking across a range of standard technical tests.

"QS technology demonstrated compelling and unique advantages during our evaluation," said Atsushi Ogawa, Chief Operating Officer, Research Center of Excellence, Honda R&D Co., Ltd. "We see potential for QS technology to add value across a range of applications, including automotive, and we are excited to move forward into the next phase of our partnership."

"Honda is a leading global automaker renowned for its engineering excellence and product quality across automotive and other applications worldwide, and its evaluation represents one of the most rigorous assessments of our technology to date," said Dr. Siva Sivaram, CEO and President of QS. "This agreement reflects the growing confidence in QS solid-state lithium-metal batteries to enable safer, higher-density energy storage."

About QuantumScape Corporation
QuantumScape is on a mission to revolutionize energy storage to enable a sustainable future. The company's next-generation solid-state lithium-metal battery technology is designed to enable greater energy density, faster charging and enhanced safety to support the transition away from legacy energy sources toward a lower carbon future. title: Rivian lays off hundreds of workers days after new vehicle deliveries begin
content:
Rivian said it's laying off hundreds of employees, or less than 2% of its workforce, as part of restructuring efforts aimed at making the company profitable for the first time.

The layoffs come one week after the Irvine-based electric vehicle maker began deliveries of its highly anticipated R2 SUV.

The company is hoping that the R2, which is currently only available as a performance version for $57,990, could attract more customers with its lower price tag.

But industry analysts said the performance R2 is still not affordable for many Americans, and investors reacted with disappointment to the first deliveries June 9, with shares falling 7% that day. On Wednesday, Rivian shares gained .33 points, or 2%, to close at $16.26.

The company said a standard version of the R2 starting at $44,990 will become available next year.

The layoffs took effect on Monday and affected Rivian's service and customer organization employees, including sales and marketing teams. Rivian employed 15,232 people as of December.

"We recently restructured a handful of teams within Rivian as we work to profitably scale our ‌business," a ⁠company spokesperson said.

The laid off employees have been provided with severance packages and are encouraged to apply for other open roles with Rivian, the company said.

Read more:Rivian to place more than 100 new EV chargers around Caruso properties

Rivian may be trying to reach profitability by saving money on labor, said Ivan Drury, director of insights at Edmunds.

"You have to wonder to what degree they do plan on replacing those people with some level of AI and automation," he said.

Rivian, which is pouring money into autonomous vehicle efforts including a robotaxi partnership with Uber, has struggled to turn a profit with its luxury EVs.

The layoffs are likely not directly tied to recent reception of the R2, auto analyst Brian Moody said.

"I think that it's declining interest in new electric cars, and maybe declining interest in expensive things," he said. "We can surmise that [layoff] process began long before the R2 launch."

The company lost $3.6 billion last year and recently said it is no longer expecting to meet its 2027 adjusted core profit target.

There has been a broad cooling of the EV market. Major automakers including Honda and Ford have cut back their EV options as excitement for the vehicles has fallen under the Trump administration. A $7,500 EV tax credit for new vehicles expired in September.

Read more:Rivian finds a way to shine even as the EV market struggles in the dark

Story Continues

Rivian cut 4.5% of its workforce in October, or more than 600 jobs, following the expiration of the credit. The company also laid off about 200 employees in September.

In a recent turnaround, Rivian surprised the market with strong earnings results in February, reporting gross profits for 2025 of $144 million compared with a net loss in 2024 of $1.2 billion. Gross profit is revenue without subtracting the cost of production expenses.

In its earnings release, Rivian credited the swing to "strong software and services performance, higher average selling prices, and reductions in cost per vehicle."

"The company has never posted a full year's worth of profit, and this is the one lever they can pull to rightsize things," Drury said.
